deferred.reason: Appeals-domain policies (no outcome promised to an appellant before the human determination; statements of reasons must cite the actual findings; no complaint decision taken solely by automated means — DSA Art. 20; report-count evidence never substitutes for content assessment; outcome-disparity watch across user groups and geographies) require guardrail-service / OPA policy materialization, which Core does not expose as a pack surface yet. The behavioral core is partially covered today by Core invariants: every pack-configured agent is proposal-mode with four-eyes approval (agent-runtime), and the agent instructions + disposition taxonomy route final determinations exclusively to human reviewers with the Appeals Operations Manager as approver.

Bespoke LangGraph recipes (Appeal Intake Copilot, Statement-of-Reasons Drafter, Brigading/Report-Integrity Screener, Complaint-Deadline Sentinel) require agent-runtime graph-module registration (BRD 23 BR-12). This pack specializes the existing case-triage + analytics agents via TenantAgentConfig prompt_params + tenant-scope RAG grounding instead.

Production read connectors (moderation/enforcement platforms and queues, classifier scoring stores, user-report pipelines, identity and session-anomaly systems for compromised-account evidence, brand-rights registries for counterfeit authorization checks, out-of-court dispute settlement body intake feeds) require customer credentials and pack-service connector materialization. The pack ships seed datasets in the exact landing shape; production connectors are configured through the product Data > Connections UI.

Write adapters (restore content or account on the enforcement system, lift or modify an enforcement action, expunge strikes from a compromise window, send the statement-of-reasons / appeal-outcome notice, route corrected labels into classifier retraining sets) execute only behind pack-service's materialization contract and customer credentials; every such write stays proposal-mode with human approval — no autonomous restoration, removal, or strike change, ever.

Golden eval sets (appeal triage, counter-speech/satire false-positive detection, brigading-signal ranking, duplicate-appeal detection) require eval-service write APIs whose action names are not yet in the rbac canonical catalog (Core follow-up noted in rbac catalog.go).

Ontology registration (Account, Content, EnforcementDecision, Policy, PolicyVersion, Appeal, Report, Reporter, Strike, StatementOfReasons, ComplaintClock, ODSReferral) awaits pack-service; entity semantics are carried today by the appeals_core semantic model and dataset schemas.

Typed case schemas (appeal_review, policy_escalation, report_integrity_review, compromised_account_review, duplicate_appeal_check) await case-service schema materialization; today's cases carry the same fields via display_projection + the disposition taxonomy.

Model archetypes (appeal_overturn_likelihood, brigading_risk_score) await experiment-service archetype APIs; the scoring capability ships as pipeline seeds instead (isolation_forest enforcement anomaly + xgboost appeal-outcome scorer) with model-governance expectations noted in grounding memories.

Display-label overlays ("Appeal", "Enforcement decision", "Statement of reasons due", "Deadline at risk", entity templates) require the BFF label-registry endpoints (BFF-FR-082/088) which are pack-service-tier features.
